name: treefy

metadata.name: treefy
metadata.summary: A dynamic tree library for algorithmic problem solving

documentation:
# Treefy - A Binary Tree Library

Treefy is a simple yet flexible Python library designed to help you easily work with binary trees. It allows you to define custom functions and perform a variety of tree operations like insertion, traversal, height calculation, and more.

## Features

- **BinaryTree** class for working with binary trees.
- **TreeNode** class to represent individual nodes.
- **Custom function registration** allowing users to define and use custom functions.
- **Tree Operations**:
  - Preorder, Inorder, and Postorder traversals.
  - Level order traversal (breadth-first search).
  - Height calculation of the tree.
  - Drawing a textual representation of the tree.
- **Attach Subtree**: Attach a whole tree/subtree as a left or right child.
- **Support for DSA Problems**: You can register custom DSA functions like "maximum path sum" or "LCA" (Lowest Common Ancestor) and solve problems dynamically.

## Basic Usage

With this project, there is one test.py, which includes the usage of BinaryTree class, how to add nodes, traversals etc
In addition to that, there are two custom functions provided: Max Path Sum and LCA for your reference

## Installation

Clone the repository or download the source files and place them in your project directory. Alternatively, you can install it via pip (if the package is uploaded to PyPI):

```bash
pip install treefy

methods.name: treefy.tree.TreeNode.is_leaf
documentation: Returns True if the node is a leaf (no children).

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.__init__
documentation: Initializes the tree with an optional root node.

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.add_node
documentation:
Adds a node to the tree as a left or right child of the given parent node.
\:param parent_node: TreeNode to which the new node will be attached
\:param value: Value of the new node
\:param is_left: If True, the new node is added as the left child

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.attach_subtree
documentation:
Attaches a subtree to the specified parent node in the current tree.

```
param parent

Node in the current tree to which the subtree will be attached.

param subtree

Subtree to attach. Can be either a TreeNode or a Tree (BinaryTree) object.

param is_left

Boolean indicating whether to attach the subtree as the left or right child.

return

None
```

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.generate_tree_from_array
documentation:
Function to generate a binary tree from a list (array).
This assumes a complete binary tree representation.
\:param arr: List of values representing the binary tree in level order.
\:return: The root of the generated tree.

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.register_function
documentation: Allows users to add their custom function.

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.get_height
documentation:
Calculates the height of the tree/subtree.
\:param node: Starting node for calculating height (defaults to the root)

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.level_order_traversal
documentation:
Performs a level-order traversal (breadth-first traversal) of the tree.
Returns a list of node values in level order.

methods.name: treefy.tree.BinaryTree.print_tree
documentation: Helper function to print the tree drawing.
